rows.q: In Alphabet's 2023 10-K, what percentage of consolidated revenues were international in 2023?

A.answer: Alphabet stated that “**International revenues accounted for approximately 53% of our consolidated revenues in 2023**.” [goog-20231231.pdf p.15]

B.answer: Alphabet’s 2023 10-K states: “**International revenues accounted for approximately 53% of our consolidated revenues in 2023**.” [goog-20231231.pdf p.15]

rows.q: In Alphabet's 2023 10-K, roughly what share of total revenues came from online advertising in 2023?

A.answer:
- **Google advertising:** **$237,855 million** [goog-20231231.pdf p.36]  
- **Total revenues:** **$307,394 million** [goog-20231231.pdf p.36]  
- **Share of total revenues:** **0.7738**, or about **77%** [goog-20231231.pdf p.36]

_Note: I removed one or more statements I could not verify against the source documents. I'm not stating anything I couldn't confirm._

B.answer:
Roughly **77%** of Alphabet’s 2023 total revenues came from online advertising, based on **Google advertising of $237,855 million** divided by **total revenues of $307,394 million** in 2023 [goog-20231231.pdf p.36].

Alphabet also states in prose: **“We generated more than 75% of total revenues from online advertising in 2023.”** [goog-20231231.pdf p.12]

rows.q: In Alphabet's 2023 10-K, how do the rights of the different classes of common/capital stock differ?

A.answer:
Alphabet states: “The rights of the holders of each class of our common and capital stock are identical, except with respect to voting.” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]

- **Class A common stock:** “Each share of Class A common stock is entitled to one vote per share.” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]
- **Class B common stock:** “Each share of Class B common stock is entitled to 10 votes per share.” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]
- **Class C capital stock:** “Class C capital stock has no voting rights, except as required by applicable law.” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]

Alphabet also states: “Shares of Class B common stock may be converted at any time at the option of the stockholder and automatically convert upon sale or transfer to Class A common stock.” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]

For economic rights, Alphabet states: “The rights, including the liquidation and dividend rights, of the holders of our Class A, Class B, and Class C stock are identical, except with respect to voting.” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]

B.answer:
In Alphabet’s 2023 10-K, the three classes differ **only in voting rights**; otherwise, their rights are the same. Alphabet states: “**The rights of the holders of each class of our common and capital stock are identical, except with respect to voting.**” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]

Specifically:
- **Class A common stock:** “**Each share of Class A common stock is entitled to one vote per share.**” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]
- **Class B common stock:** “**Each share of Class B common stock is entitled to 10 votes per share.**” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]
- **Class C capital stock:** “**Class C capital stock has no voting rights, except as required by applicable law.**” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]

Additional distinction for Class B:
- “**Shares of Class B common stock may be converted at any time at the option of the stockholder and automatically convert upon sale or transfer to Class A common stock.**” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]

On economic rights, the 10-K says: “**The rights, including the liquidation and dividend rights, of the holders of our Class A, Class B, and Class C stock are identical, except with respect to voting.**” [goog-20231231.pdf p.81]
